source.section: 3 - Air-to-Air Weapons > 3.2 - AIM-9M Sidewinder > 3.2.2 - AIM-9M Sidewinder (Radar)

steps.action: Set IR COOL switch to NORM. Infrared seeker in the AIM-9 nose must be cooled down to increase sensitivity and reduce background noise. There is enough coolant for 3 hours.
Weapon Select Switch – SIDEWINDER (DOWN), or « LSHIFT+S »
Master Mode switch will be automatically set to A/A
When SIDEWINDER is selected and radar is operating, press the Sensor Control Switch FWD to select ACM (Air Combat Maneuvering) radar mode
Select ACM sub-mode using the Sensor Select switch again. We will select BST sub-mode.

Fly to place target in dashed circle on the HUD to lock it on radar when at 5 nm or closer. When target is flying through this scan zone, it is automatically locked on to in STT (Single Target Track) mode
Press and hold the Cage/Uncage switch (« C » by default) to uncage the Sidewinder.

Fly to place the Steering Dot inside the ASE/NIRD (Allowable Steering Error Normalized In-Range Display) Circle squeeze trigger (« Spacebar ») when you see the SHOOT cue over the TD (Target Designation) box on the HUD to launch missile.
